real_insight: There is a real and named engineering pathology — the 'this is MY code' / 'I am the engineer who shipped this' identity-bond to artifact — that the verse precisely names with nirmama and nirahaṅkāra. The verse does not require absence of contribution; it requires absence of the I-claim on the contribution. Shipped artifact and operator identity are uncoupled. The operational artifact is the senior author who reads the new senior's redesign on its merits, finds two genuine technical problems, names them flatly, and reviews the launch under his name with quiet attention.
objection: The 'shipped artifact and operator identity are uncoupled' framing could lapse into wisdom-theater — but the analog earns it by naming the specific engineering scenario where the pathology fires (a four-year code authorship being redesigned by a new senior) and giving the falsifiable artifact (the original author finds two genuine durability-guarantee problems with the Redis approach and names them as the engineer who happens to know the historical context, not as authority defending territory). The 'durability guarantee under network partition' is a real, named technical concern — the failure mode of Redis-based deduplication that the Postgres-row approach prevents — that any senior would recognise as legitimate engineering content, not territorial defense. This separates the verse's reading from the misread 'be a doormat about your work'. Pass.
